feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: CAP Node.js: Add authorization filter without breaking OData options
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working with SAP CAP (Node.js) exposing OData services consumed by a SAPUI5 SmartTable.

I have a data model with a many-to-many relationship:

  • A MainEntity can have N SubEntities
  • A SubEntity can belong to M MainEntities

Example (simplified):

entity MainEntity {
  key ID : UUID;
  name   : String;
  subs   : Composition of many MainSub on subs.main = $self;
}

entity MainSub {
  key ID   : UUID;
  main     : Association to MainEntity;
  subCode  : String;
}

 Requirement

I need to implement row-level authorization in the backend such that:

A user can only see MainEntities that are linked to SubEntities assigned to that user.

Conceptually:

WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM MainSub s
  WHERE s.main_ID = MainEntity.ID
    AND s.subCode IN (userAllowedSubs)
)

Problem:

The frontend (SmartTable) sends dynamic OData queries like:

$filter=contains(name,'ABC')
&$orderby=name asc
&$top=100
&$skip=0

If I override the query in a before READ handler like:

req.query.SELECT.where = ...

 I break the original OData query, losing filtering, sorting, paging, etc.

I’m looking for the correct CAP pattern to:

  • Inject an authorization restriction (AND condition)
  • Preserve all incoming OData query options:
    • $filter
    • $orderby
    • $top / $skip
    • $select
  • Work correctly with SAPUI5 SmartTable

Expected behavior

Final query should behave like:

WHERE
  <original OData filter>
  AND EXISTS (authorization condition)

Additional context

This is conceptually similar to row-level authorization in SAP ERP (e.g. restricting data visibility based on organizational assignments), but implemented in CAP.

Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in CAP to append conditions to the existing CQN instead of replacing it?
  2. Is using req.query.where(...) with EXISTS the correct approach?
  3. Are there better patterns (e.g. CDS views, aspects, annotations) for this type of authorization?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!!

entry.title: Bug regression regarding WSL in SAP CDS Language Support VSCODE extension
entry.content:
After updating the CDS Language support extension from 9.8 to 9.9 in vscode, the type generation isn't working anymore.
This was fixed in 9.8 but since the update it isn't working anymore.
 
It seems to execute typer in my Windows, not the WSL environment. Error message:
Could not (re)generate types for model. /usr/bin/env: ‘node’: No such file or directory
 
Is this a known problem or is there a workaround aside from downgrading to 9.8?

entry.title: Deploy initial data from CSV on a field with @readonly annotation
entry.content:
Hi CAP community,

I am trying to update our CDS model by using @readonly annotation for one of our fields. 

Based on provided documentation Declarative Constraints | capire , I've understood that this will permit to ignore the field value on both Create and Update operation.

My question here is: will this impact deploying initial data for that field for that field using CSV file ? should I expect a regeneration of this table artifact with a different column definition when using @readonly annotation?

BR,

Tesnim

entry.title: CAP CLI: "CF org not found" error during cds bind or hybrid watch despite correct cf target
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm encountering a strange issue with the SAP Cloud SDK / CAP CLI where I cannot bind my local project to Cloud Foundry services. Everything was working fine recently, but suddenly I am blocked by an "Org not found" error.

The Problem:
When I run cds watch --profile hybrid or cds bind db --to shiftroster-db, the CLI returns:
CF org  ******* not found!

What I have verified:

CF Target: Running cf target shows that I am correctly logged in and targeted to the exact Org and Space mentioned in the error.

Permissions: I have confirmed I have Space Developer and Administrator roles in the BTP Cockpit for this subaccount.

Services: Running cf services successfully lists my shiftroster-db (HANA HDI-shared) instance as create succeeded.

Troubleshooting tried:

Logged out and logged back in via cf login.

Deleted .cdsrc-private.json and attempted to re-bind.

Updated @sap/cds-dk to the latest version.

The issue persists even in a brand-new CAP project.

entry.title: Calling SAP CAP API protected by SAP IAS from ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a SAP CAP app that uses IAS for authentication and authorization. The app is deployed on BTP and provides some UI5 apps via Workzone. This all works fine. The users have AMS capabilities assigned and can access the apps and call corresponding backend services. 

The app provides APIs that will be called from ABAP AS. ABAP will have RFC destination that points to API endpoints and will use oAuth2 for authentication. The destination itself will be consumed by ABAP programs. I would like to have two technical users that will have different AMS capabilities assigned. Here I am struggling how to define these users. I tried the following approaches.

1. In IAS, I generated a cert in section Client Authentication / Certificates of my app. I used it to setup oAuth2 profile in ABAP system. I am able to get an access token and authentication works fine. The problem is with authorization. API logs the following error "403 - Error: User 'system' is lacking required roles: ". The problem is clear to me but I have no idea how to assign AMS capabilities to this technical user.

2. I created a technical user in SAP IAS and assigned AMS capabilties to this user. The problem here is I have no idea how to use this user in oAuth2 profile in ABAP AS. If I have an IAS user, how can I use username/password of this user to call API.

The documentation seems to be really fragmented, having XSUAA and IAS does not help. How can this simple requirement of consuming CAP API from ABAP via technical user can be achieved? Is there a good tutorial for this?

Thanks

entry.title: Setting HTTP credentials after upgrade to CDS Test to 1.0
entry.content:
Hi,

has anyone upgraded to CDS test version 1.0 and is still able to set username/password during integration tests? I asked on Github but no lock there.

https://github.com/cap-js/cds-test/issues/83

Thanks

entry.title: How to properly logout from a CAP mocked authentication?
entry.content:
Given a CAP application using mocked authentication

"cds": {
    "requires": {
      "[mocked]": {
        "auth": {
          "kind": "mocked",
          "users": {
            "adm": {
              "password": "1234",
              "roles": [
                "admin"
              ]
            },
            "user": {
              "password": "1234",
              "roles": [
                "readonly"
              ]
            }
          }
        },

 When I run the service locally, the popup requesting user + password is displayed. However, it is "never" requested again and there isn't any obvious way for "logout" from the mocked user. Even if I kill the app and re-run 'cds serve' or 'cds watch', the latest authentication is somehow reused.

I checked in DevTools the session and local storage and there is nothing. 

There is cookie that is generated, but if I delete it, it not only kills the session but also the BAS session (kinda I am forced to reload BAS). After BAS is reloaded (just a refresh on the page, no need to restart the dev space) and then rerun the service, the popup is displayed again and I am able to test with a different user.

Conclusion: somehow the authentication is linked with the BAS session, which clearly is not ideal. 

How to properly logout from a CAP mocked authentication without having to kill the browser cookies?

entry.title: Missing java code in remote service tutorial
entry.content:
Hello,
It seems the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/remote-service-extend.html only has nodeJs code.
How to find a java version of that ?
Regards

entry.title: Roadmap for BTP CAPM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Glad to be part of this group and learn from experienced professionals.

I am currently working as an SAP Business ByDesign Technical Consultant and would like to start learning SAP BTP technologies such as CAPM, Fiori, SAP HANA Database, and SAP UI5 to become a full-stack SAP BTP developer.

Could you please guide me on:

Which topic should I start with first?

What would be the best learning path?

Any recommended resources, courses, or practice methods for clear understanding?

Your suggestions and guidance would be very helpful for my learning journey.

Regards,
Tirumala

entry.title: Transaction handling in CAP within try-catch and a loop
entry.content:
How does CAP handle DB errors that happen inside of try-catch clauses inside of a loop?

Below is my sample code. Assume only order #2 fails when updating "Users" and catch engages.

What will be saved?

What I observed in our real application (when we got a deadlock due to concurrent update to "Users"): book "b2" and user "u2" wouldn't be updated and catch was engaged. But then order "2" would be updated (obviously because it happens after the first loop). As if each try-catch would have its own transaction scope. It looked like all other objects (from order 1 and order 3) were updated too.

When I tested in local environment with SQLite -> the catch did not engage, everything ran without exceptions but then the whole request would fail with 500 and nothing would be saved. As if it remembers that the whole request is poisoned and fails in the very end. I assume handling is different when using HANA. I must mention that locally I simulated error differently (by trying to set the ID to an already used value). I am not sure how to simulate concurrent writes from multiple instances in a local environment...

 

this.on('test', async (req) => {

  const goodOrders = [];
  const badOrders = [];
  for (const order of req.data.value) {
    try {
      await cds.update('Books', order.bookID).with({ stock: { '+=': -1 } });
      goodOrders.push(order);
      // Assume "deadlock exception" for user "u2" from order "2".
      await cds.update('Users', order.userID).with({ totalBooks: { '+=': 1 } });
    } catch (error) {
      badOrders.push(order);
    }
  }

  for (const goodOrder of goodOrders) {
    await cds.update('Orders', goodOrder).with({ state: 'OK' });
  }

  return {
    goodOrders,
    badOrders,
  }
})

POST /examples:

{
  "value": [
    { "ID": "1", "bookID": "b1", "userID": "u1" },
    { "ID": "2", "bookID": "b2", "userID": "u2-error!!!" },
    { "ID": "3", "bookID": "b3", "userID": "u3"}
  ]
}

 I would appreciate any links to documentation. The CAP doc is quite sparse around transaction handling...

entry.title: SAP UI5 with SAP CAP error when using Typescript. Component.js fails to load
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm recently getting an error with new projects created with these features:

  • SAP CAP
  • SAP Ui5 Basic app inside /app
  • Typescript for both service and ui5

The error is happening when I run the project via "cds watch", and enter to the UI app, nothing loads, and in the browser console I have an error. The error message is the following:

Log-dbg.js:497 2026-06-12 10:58:08.031500 [FUTURE FATAL] Failed to load component for container container - ModuleError: failed to load 'contracts/Component.js' from ./Component.js: script load error  
 ModuleError: failed to load 'contracts/Component.js' from ./Component.js: script load error
    at xe (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.146.0/resources/sap-ui-core.js:10:7011)
    at pe.failWith (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.146.0/resources/sap-ui-core.js:10:4799)
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.146.0/resources/sap-ui-core.js:10:10858)
Caused by: Error: script load error
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.146.0/resources/sap-ui-core.js:10:10900)Caused by: Error: script load error
    at HTMLScriptElement.i (ui5loader-dbg.js:1490:55)

 Looks like something is going on in the translation from ts to js, because its failing to load component.js. The content of the component is the following:

import BaseComponent from "sap/ui/core/UIComponent";
import { createDeviceModel } from "./model/models";

/**
 * @namespace contracts
 */
export default class Component extends BaseComponent {

    public static metadata = {
        manifest: "json",
        interfaces: [
            "sap.ui.core.IAsyncContentCreation"
        ]
    };

    public init(): void {
        // call the base component's init function
        super.init();

        // set the device model
        this.setModel(createDeviceModel(), "device");

        // enable routing
        this.getRouter().initialize();
    }
}

These is my package.json in CAP:

{
  "name": "project",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@cap-js/hana": "^2",
    "@sap/cds": "^9",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-typer": ">=0.1",
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.16.0",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^2.4",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^9",
    "@types/node": "^24.0.0",
    "cds-plugin-ui5": "^0.13.0",
    "tsx": "^4",
    "typescript": "^6.0.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve",
    "watch-contracts": "cds watch --open contracts/index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false --livereload false"
  },
  "private": true,
  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "[production]": {
        "auth": "xsuaa"
      }
    }
  },
  "imports": {
    "#cds-models/*": "./@cds-models/*/index.js"
  },
  "workspaces": [
    "app/*"
  ]
}

and this is my package.json in ui5 app:

{
  "name": "projecty",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "An SAP Fiori application.",
  "keywords": [
    "ui5",
    "openui5",
    "sapui5"
  ],
  "main": "webapp/index.html",
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {
    "@ui5/cli": "^4.0.33",
    "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling": "1",
    "@sap-ux/eslint-plugin-fiori-tools": "^10.0.0",
    "eslint": "^10",
    "@sapui5/types": "~1.148.0",
    "ui5-tooling-transpile": "^3.10.0",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "deploy-config": "npx -p @SAP/ux-ui5-tooling fiori add deploy-config cf"
  }
}

Ive tried to downgrading versions comparing to a project that runs, but nothing works. Issue is happening with all projects created from generator.

entry.title: Production Logging Strategy for Client-Side UI5 Application Deployed on SAP BTP
entry.content:
We are building an app using Ui5 freestyle that acts as a side-by-side extension in Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 (SSCv2).

Our goal is to add logging features to the app to assist developers in debugging and troubleshooting issues in the production environment.

Since we are not creating any backend services, we cannot utilize the Cloud logging services in BTP. This app is purely a client-side Ui5 application.

Could you please advise on how to implement logging within our app so that developers can access the logs when the app is deployed on BTP and running in SSCv2 as a mashup

entry.title: The custom extension section (XML fragment) in Object Page is always displayed at the end
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

The title basically describes the problem I'm facing, and at this point, I'm not sure if it's due to a misconfiguration in my project (or a misconfiguration), or a bug.

Here's my scenario: I have an SAP CAP project (Node.js) with several SAP Fiori Elements apps running on SAP BTP. The app where I'm having the problem uses the List Report - Object Page pattern and incorporates some extensions. In the Object Page, besides the header section, I have an item table (defined with CDS annotations), and a completely custom section (defined in an XML Fragment) with several UI5 controls (ObjectStatus, Texts, Inputs, etc.). I need the custom section to appear before the item table. So far, I haven't been able to achieve this.

Below is a snippet from the manifest.json file containing the configuration:

//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

                "BatchesObjectPage": {
                    "type": "Component",
                    "id": "BatchesObjectPage",
                    "name": "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage",
                    "options": {
                        "settings": {
                            "editableHeaderContent": false,
                            "contextPath": "/Batches",
                            "controlConfiguration": {
                                "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.FieldGroup#BOX_GEN_DATA": {
                                    "fields": {
                                        "ClientSideBoxesSummary": {
                                            "template": "packaginglabelfull.ext.fragment.BoxesSummaryFacet",
                                            "position": {
                                                "placement": "After",
                                                "anchor": "DataField::itemsPerBox"
                                            }
                                        }
                                    }
                                },
                                "items/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
                                    "tableSettings": {
                                        "quickVariantSelection": {
                                            "paths": [
                                                {
                                                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionVariant#AllBItemsSelVariant"
                                                },
                                                {
                                                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionVariant#PackedBItemsSelVariant"
                                                }
                                            ],
                                            "hideTableTitle": false,
                                            "showCounts": true
                                        },
                                        "disableCopyToClipboard": true,
                                        "enableExport": false,
                                        "enableFullScreen": false,
                                        "enablePaste": false,
                                        "personalization": {
                                            "column": false
                                        }
                                    }
                                }
                            },
                            "content": {
                                "footer": {
                                    "actions": {
                                        "cancelLivePackaging": {
                                            "press": ".extension.packaginglabelfull.ext.controller.BatchesObjectPageExt.cancelLivePackaging",
                                            "visible": true,
                                            "enabled": true,
                                            "text": "{i18n>CancelLivePackaging}"
                                        },
                                        "finishCurrBox": {
                                            "press": ".extension.packaginglabelfull.ext.controller.BatchesObjectPageExt.finishCurrBox",
                                            "visible": true,
                                            "enabled": "{= ${supportsIncompleteBatchFinishing} }",
                                            "text": "{@i18n>FinishCurrBox}"
                                        },
                                        "reprintLastBarcodeLabels": {
                                            "press": ".extension.packaginglabelfull.ext.controller.BatchesObjectPageExt.reprintLastBarcodeLabels",
                                            "visible": true,
                                            "enabled": true,
                                            "text": "{@i18n>ReprintLastBarcodeLabels}"
                                        },
                                        "takeWiPItemWeight": {
                                            "press": ".extension.packaginglabelfull.ext.controller.BatchesObjectPageExt.takeWiPItemWeight",
                                            "visible": true,
                                            "enabled": "{ext>/f3Enabled}",
                                            "text": "[F3] Tomar Peso"
                                        }
                                    }
                                },
                                "body": {
                                    "sections": {
                                        "LivePackaging": {
                                            "type": "XMLFragment",
                                            "template": "packaginglabelfull.ext.fragment.LivePackagingSection",
                                            "title": "{@i18n>LivePackaging}",
                                            "position": {
                                                "anchor": "items::LineItem",
                                                "placement": "Before"
                                            }
                                        }
                                    }
                                }
                            },
                            "navigation": {
                                "items": {
                                    "detail": {
                                        "route": "BatchItemsObjectPage"
                                    }
                                }
                            },
                            "enableLazyLoading": true
                        }
                    }
                },
//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
 

The important sections are "items/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem" and "LivePackaging" (which I've highlighted in blue).

 

Any help or comments are welcome and greatly appreciated.

entry.title: @sap/cds-rfc: RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE (NIECONN_BROKEN) in BAS using cds bind --exec, but works aft
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am trying to run a CAP Node.js application locally in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) using hybrid mode (cds bind --exec). The application connects to an on-premise SAP system via Destination Service + Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector using @SAP/cds-rfc.

The exact same application works perfectly when deployed to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, but fails when run locally in BAS.

Environment

  • SAP Business Application Studio

  • Node.js: 22.13.1

  • @SAP/cds: 9.9.1

  • @SAP/cds-dk: 9.9.1

  • @SAP/cds-rfc: 2.2.1

  • @SAP-rfc/node-rfc-library: 1.1.2

  • @SAP-cloud-sdk/connectivity: 4.7.0

Hybrid setup

Service bindings are created successfully:

cds bind --to navisaccess-auth
cds bind --to navisaccess-connectivity
cds bind --to navisaccess-destination

Running:

cds bind --exec -- node rfctest.js

correctly injects all Cloud Foundry service bindings.

Minimal reproducible example

const cds = require("@sap/cds");

(async () => {
    try {
        console.log("Connecting...");

        const client = await cds.connect.to("P23_ABAP", {
            credentials: {
                destination: "P23_ABAP"
            }
        });

        console.log("Connected.");

        console.log("Calling RFC_PING...");

        const result = await client.send("RFC_PING", {});

        console.log(result);
    } catch (err) {
        console.error(err);
    }
})();

Output

Connecting...
Connected.
Calling RFC_PING...

RFCException NIECONN_BROKEN :
unable to receive data from connectivityproxy.internal.cf.ap10.hana.ondemand.com:20001

RFC_COMMUNICATION_FAILURE

Stack trace points to:

RFCService.begin()
createBapiClientConnectionHandler()
RFCClientAddonConnectionHandler.open()

What I have verified

  • cds bind works successfully.

  • Destination Service binding is successful.

  • Connectivity Service binding is successful.

  • XSUAA binding is successful.

  • cds.connect.to() succeeds.

  • The destination is resolved successfully.

  • The RFC client is created successfully.

  • The failure occurs only when the first RFC call (RFC_PING or GET_SYSTEM_TIME_REMOTE) is executed.

  • The exact same code works after deploying the application to Cloud Foundry using cf push.

  • It has has never been tested locally in BAS.

My question

Is RFC access through @SAP/cds-rfc + SAP Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector officially supported when running locally using cds bind --exec (hybrid mode)?

If yes, is there any additional configuration required for BAS or hybrid execution?

Or is RFC communication through Cloud Connector only supported for applications running inside the Cloud Foundry runtime?

Any guidance or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: AMS: can authorization policies be combined/shared across multiple CAP apps?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm exploring how AMS handles authorization across several independent CAP apps. Each app has its own IAS application and deploys its own DCL base policies. My goal is to understand whether one assignment can cover policies from multiple apps.

What I tried:

  1. Reuse service – From the documentation I couldn't work out how a CAP app is actually linked to a reuse service. Setting consumed-services on the consuming app's IAS instance had no effect: the reuse app didn't appear under "Reuse services consumed by this application", and no reuse dropdown showed in the consuming app's Authorization Policies tab.
  2. Combine Authorization Policies (Add Combination) – Only policies from the same application are selectable.

The docs say a combined policy can "bundle authorization policies within or across solutions":

Questions:

  • Is combining/sharing policies across applications supported for custom CAP apps, or only for SAP-delivered bundled solutions?
  • If supported, what establishes the relationship so another app's base policies become selectable?
  • If not, is the intended pattern to centralize assignment via Identity Directory groups (SCIM2/IPS)?

entry.title: Add user roles is outdated in cap tutorial
entry.content:
Following the tutorial "Create a CAP Service with BAS Productivity Tools", the step 5 - Add user role is outdated, because the person icon is not showing in service, like step shows.

Could sap update this step and show how to provide same control over service, please.

This affects next tutorial "Test CAP Service and its Destination", where I have to test the service and get 403 error

entry.title: Problem in extending V4 RAP service to List Report UI via CAPM Layer
entry.content:
I have created a Managed scenario in RAP and used V4 service binding. Then I created a CAP project in BAS consuming that V4 service and metadata. In service.cds and service.js too, I didn't added any custom functionality, just basic entity projection in service.cds and basic tx function for all methods (CREATE, READ, UPDATE and DELETE) in service.js as I want to use the CRUD functionalities offered by Managed RAP scenario directly. 

Then I created a List Report Application using Local CAP Project and consuming this CAP project mentioned above. 

Now when I am previewing this application, no data is showing in the List Report by clicking GO button. If I click on Create Button, Object page is always in busy state and never loads. But If I load the Fiori Preview in RAP, all CRUD operations works fine. 

In the Console, these errors come while loading the site from cds watch in BAS - 

Can anyone help what else should be done and where? And CAP layer is required for my Requirement as I have to implement roles and securities and other security related stuff. Directly coonecting FIori App to local system and consuming service is not a feasible solution for me

entry.title: Problem with discarding entity using CAP as a proxy of RAP V4 Service
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

i've built an App on the Public Cloud, using RAP V4 Service for data persistence and also using determinations, validations etc.
I'm then using a CAP Service to add custom functionality with accessing external/SAP API's. I added my RAP V4 Service using basic entity projection and a little more complicated event handlers. Most of it works good when consumed by Fiori Elements App, but i am facing an issue, where i discard the entity i am editing (not when creating), it successfully discard its, but then fails to read and rebind to the "active" Entity.

 

From the UI Console and Network Tab, aswell as debugging for hours i'm certain that the discard action works, but the two GET requests fired by FE are running on errors.

My guess to what happens is, that the Draft is discarded before the Read triggers, therefore Entity with "isActiveEntity"=false cannot be accessed and the query fails. The weird thing about that is, that this should be dafault Draft Logic and works everywhere else. So i feel like i am missing something. 

Also i am not using @odata.draft.enabled, because that would then use the CAP Default but we have all our data on the RAP Service and need to proxy the requests the way i do (please correct me if i'm wrong here).

Help is greatly appreciated and i will provide any further information you might need.

Cheers, Henrik

entry.title: SAP Build Code BAS -/ui5-create-app fails with "@ui5/mcp-server" error and Accept Application button
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to create a freestyle SAPUI5 application in SAP Build Code (BAS) using the new Joule command:
/ui5-create-app

Prompt used

/ui5-create-app Create a freestyle SAPUI5 application for the Manage Sales Order CAP service.
Use the OData V4 service:
/service/manage_Sales_Order_App

CAP Project Information
I already have a CAP project generated successfully using Joule.
Project structure:
db/
srv/
app/
The OData service is available and generated correctly.

Joule Response
Joule returns the following proposal:
Let's configure your Manage Sales Order app using JavaScript instead of TypeScript.
Namespace: com.myorg.managesalesorder
Base Path:
/home/user/projects/Manage_Sales_Order_App/app
Create App Directory: true
OData V4 URL:
service/manage_Sales_Order_App/
OData Entity Set:
SalesOrders
TypeScript: false
Once you're ready, you can accept the application proposal
by pressing the "Accept Application" button.
Issue
The "Accept Application" button never appears.

At the same time, I can see the following error in the BAS Error panel:
Failed to generate app proposal:

Cannot find package '@ui5/mcp-server'
imported from

/extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0/dist/extension-min.cjs

Did you mean to import
"@ui5/mcp-server/lib/api.js"?

Source:
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development

Troubleshooting Performed
I have already tried:

Refreshing BAS browser window
Opening a new Joule conversation
Using a simplified /ui5-create-app prompt
Creating a new Development Space
Reopening the project

The same error occurs every time.

Environment
SAP Build Code / SAP Business Application Studio
SAP Fiori Tools - Guided Development
Extension version reported in error:
sap-ux-help-extension-1.28.0
Questions
Is this a known issue with the SAP Fiori Tools / UI5 Create Application assistant?
Has anyone encountered the missing package error:
@ui5/mcp-server
Is there any workaround available until the issue is fixed?
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: @sap/cds was loaded from different locations
entry.content:
Environment

  • npm: 11.12.1
  • Node.js: 24.13.1
  • @SAP/cds-dk (global): 10.0.5

Hello,

I'm running into a very strange issue with a newly created CAP project, and I'm hoping someone can shed some light on what's happening.

The project is basically a bare CAP application. If it only contains the backend service, everything works fine. However, as soon as I add a generated Fiori application, the server fails to start with the following error:

$ npm start

> nai-rtr-budget-mgmt@1.0.0 start
> cds serve

[cds-plugin-ui5] [INFO] Running cds-plugin-ui5@0.13.6 (@sap/cds-dk@9.9.3, @SAP/cds@9.9.3)
[cds-plugin-ui5] [INFO] Mounting /budgetadmin to UI5 app C:\Dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\app\budget-admin (id=budget-admin)
[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

[cds] [INFO] connect to db > sqlite { url: 'db.sqlite' }
[cds] [INFO] using auth strategy {
  kind: 'mocked',
  impl: 'node_modules\\@sap\\cds\\lib\\srv\\middlewares\\auth\\basic-auth.js'
}
[cds] [INFO] serving BudgetService {
  at: [ '/odata/v4/budget' ],
  decl: 'srv\\budget-service.cds:3',
  impl: 'node_modules\\@sap\\cds\\srv\\app-service.js'
}
info fiori-tools-proxy Using UI5 version 1.150.0 based on manifest.json
info fiori-tools-proxy Starting fiori-tools-proxy using following configuration:
info fiori-tools-proxy proxy: 'undefined'
info fiori-tools-proxy ignoreCertErrors: 'false'
info fiori-tools-proxy backend: undefined
info fiori-tools-proxy ui5: [{"path":"/resources","url":"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com","version":"1.150.0"},{"path":"/test-resources","url":"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com","version":"1.150.0"}]
info fiori-tools-proxy debug: 'false'
info fiori-tools-appreload Livereload middleware started for port 35729 and path C:\Dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\app\budget-admin\webapp
(node:29036) [DEP0190] DeprecationWarning: Passing args to a child process with shell option true can lead to security vulnerabilities, as the arguments are not escaped, only concatenated.
(Use `node --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created)
[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

info fiori-tools-preview Initialized for app budgetadmin
[cds-plugin-ui5] [ERROR] Failed to inject application pages to welcome page! The links of the application pages may not work properly!

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
ERROR: @SAP/cds was loaded from different locations:
 
  c:\dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\node_modules\@sap\cds
  ~\appdata\roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\node_modules\@sap\cds 

Ensure a single install to avoid hard-to-resolve errors.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

I can work around the issue by uninstalling the globally installed @SAP/cds-dk, but that doesn't seem like a real solution.

What's confusing is that I have another CAP project on the same machine that also uses cds-plugin-ui5, and it works perfectly regardless of whether @SAP/cds-dk is installed globally or not. I also noticed that project even has @SAP/cds-dk installed locally as a dev dependency, yet it still runs without any issues.

Here are the things I've already tried:

  • Matched the package versions between the working and non-working projects.
  • Downgraded the globally installed @SAP/cds-dk from v10 to v9 (since the working project was originally created with v9).
  • Installed @SAP/cds-dk locally instead of relying on the global installation.
  • Verified that the issue only occurs after adding the Fiori application. A backend-only CAP project runs fine.

None of the above made any difference. The new project continued to fail while the existing project continued to work.

The only things I've found that make the new project work are:

  1. Completely removing any additional @SAP/cds-dk installation so only a single installation is available.

Strangely, adding a .env file containing: DEBUG=all also allows the project to start, even with the global @SAP/cds-dk installed. I don't understand why enabling debug logging would affect this behavior.

Has anyone encountered something similar before? More importantly, why would two CAP projects on the same machine behave differently when they appear to have the same tooling and dependency versions? Is there anything specific I should compare between the two projects to determine why one resolves @SAP/cds correctly while the other does not?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.
